fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Vermont CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Vermont - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity vermont, how to transfer CNA license to vermont, vermont CNA endorsement application

board.name: Vermont Secretary of State
board.mailing_address: OPR - Licensing Division, 89 Main Street, 3rd Floor, Montpelier, VT 05609-1103

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years, by September 30th of odd-numbered years

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Vermont.

steps.title: Create Account & Apply Online
steps.description: Register for an account on the Vermont Professional Regulation Portal (VPRP) and complete the online application for CNA licensure, selecting the appropriate application type (e.g., by examination or endorsement).

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $50 through the Vermont Professional Regulation Portal (VPRP) during the application process.

steps.title: Verify Training Program Completion
steps.description: For licensure by examination, arrange for your state-approved nursing assistant training program to send official verification of your program completion directly to the Vermont Office of Professional Regulation.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow instructions within the Vermont Professional Regulation Portal (VPRP) application to complete any required criminal background checks.

steps.title: Pass NNAAP Examination
steps.description: For licensure by examination, register with Pearson VUE and successfully pass the National Nurse Aide Assessment Program (NNAAP) examination.

steps.title: Receive Licensure
steps.description: Upon successful completion of all requirements and review by the Office of Professional Regulation, your CNA license will be issued and added to the Vermont CNA Registry.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Vermont CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $25. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Vermont.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Vermont?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Vermont while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a LNA in VT.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Vermont.

hero.title: Become a LNA in Vermont
hero.dek: To apply for a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) license in Vermont, candidates must submit their application through the Vermont Professional Regulation Portal (VPRP).

seo.title: How to Become a LNA in Vermont
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Vermont LNA certification.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Vermont Professional Regulation Portal (VPRP) at secure.vermont.gov/ProfessionalRegulationPortal. Click 'Register an Account' to create a new user profile with your email and password. Once registered, log in and select 'Apply for a New License' to start your CNA application.
